steps.title: Identify the general claim
steps.content: The narrator's general claim: every crew member has the fixed idea that he is doing everything. This is a trend the narrator observed across boating experiences. The underlined sentence then shifts to a specific person — Harris's notion that he alone has been working.

steps.title: Match the function
steps.content:
Harris's belief is a specific instance of the general trend (followed by another instance, George's belief). So the underlined sentence offers a specific example illustrating the narrator's observation.

B captures this. A reverses the relationship (Harris's feelings about the narrator's idea). C invents "startled" (no surprise is described). D claims Harris is resolving conflict (he's instead embodying it).

B is correct.
